result: If I had the power to change the world in a single stroke, I’d instill a shared commitment to genuine empathy—an instinctive habit of stepping into each other’s perspectives before we act or speak. I believe so many of our fiercest problems—conflict, inequality, neglect of our planet—take root in the failure to recognize one another’s full humanity. With real empathy as a foundation, the ways we approach policy, education, the economy, and even everyday conversations would shift toward solutions that honor everyone’s dignity.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
